topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged on the screen, a tiny, insistent vibration against the otherwise silent hum of the house. Liam froze, his hand hovering over the phone. It was a profile picture he didn't recognize, a curated collection of filtered sunsets and cryptic captions. His breath hitched, a shallow, quick intake of air. He knew, with a sickening certainty, that this wasn't his daughter’s usual online presence. A cold knot tightened in his stomach, pulling at his chest. He felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to snatch the device away, to erase the evidence before it could unravel everything. The silence of the room seemed to amplify the frantic drumming in his ears.

[story 2]

"Show me," Sarah said, her voice dangerously level as she held up the tablet. Maya’s shoulders slumped immediately, a visible deflation of all energy. She didn't look up, her gaze fixed on the worn pattern of the rug. Every muscle in her jaw was tight, a rigid set that spoke volumes. Sarah watched the way Maya’s fingers twitched, as if bracing for impact. The air in the living room grew thick, heavy with unspoken accusations. Maya finally lifted her eyes, and in their depths, Sarah saw a flicker of something deeply guarded, something hidden behind a carefully constructed facade.

[story 3]

I found the login details tucked deep within a rarely used folder, a digital secret buried under layers of carefully constructed normalcy. My fingers trembled as I typed the password. When the profile loaded, a wave of nausea washed over me. It was a world entirely separate from the one I thought I knew—vibrant, sharp, and utterly alien. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. My vision narrowed, focusing only on the screen, the images scrolling by, each one a tiny shard of glass digging into my composure. I didn't know what to do next; the space around me felt suddenly too small to move.
